title: ophrys scolopax subsp. orientalis
intro: Ophrys scolopax subsp. orientalis, also known as the Eastern Spotted Bee Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and swelling, and has been used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments. Ophrys scolopax subsp. orientalis can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, although it is essential to note that the plant's efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
